label: Run a long-horizon pipeline
describe: Run a long-horizon, multi-step pipeline: build a task dependency graph, dispatch sub-tasks in parallel, and resume across restarts toward one large goal. Use when the goal is too big for a single session, needs parallel workers, or must survive >24h. This is an EXECUTE capability: it launches and coordinates real background work on the user's machine, so it requires explicit grant. Provide a clear `goal`; optionally cap parallelism and set a pacing budget. It composes lower-level capabilities (plan create, task dispatch, status) — granting this workflow implies you will also be asked for its members' grants.

goal.description: The long-horizon objective to work toward.

planId.description: The pipeline plan id to track/resume.

skills.label: Running a pipeline to completion (pacing discipline)

extras._note: A hypothetical workflow source used as the protocol's worked example of kind:"workflow" + transitive grants (ADR-012). scan() must surface this workflow AND its members (orchestrator.plan.create / task.dispatch / plan.status) so the members[] above resolve to present registry entries and the transitive grant has real targets.
